Bengaluru Power Cut Hits Multiple Areas Amid Heatwave; BESCOM Issues Restoration Update
Multiple localities in Bengaluru reported power cuts on Tuesday, April 21, with outages impacting parts of Bangalore South, particularly in the western region. The disruptions are attributed to ongoing maintenance work and infrastructure issues. The Bangalore Electricity Supply Company Limited (BESCOM) has also issued an update regarding the outage linked to the '66/11KV Vrushabavathi MUSS F4 Feeder 250kVA No. 105'. Meanwhile, the city continues to reel under heatwave-like conditions.
Bengaluru Power Cut Today
There is a power interruption in " 66/11kV Vrushabavathi MUSS F4 Feeder 250kva no," and the electricity supply will be restored in the afternoon, as notified by BESCOM in a social media post while reacting to a complaint.
Bengaluru Power Cut: When Will Power Supply Be Restored?
A social media user had raised a complaint linked to a power cut in Bengaluru's Saptagiri Residency, Jnanabharati.
Reacting to the complaint on social media platform X, BESCOM wrote, "In reference to your post, As of now, there is an interruption in 66/11KV Vrushabavathi MUSS F4 Feeder 250kva no-105 Tree Trimming work, Power supply will be restored @ 14:30 Hrs (Approximately)."
Bengaluru Power Cut: How To Register a Complaint of Power Disruption?
Power cuts, wrong bills, or faulty meters can disrupt homes and workplaces across Bengaluru. Knowing how to file a complaint with BESCOM helps you restore supply and billing clarity quickly. A clear process also reduces confusion during stressful outages.
Raising issues without delay allows BESCOM staff to locate faults sooner and correct records faster. Timely complaints support more accurate maintenance planning and billing data. When many consumers report problems, BESCOM can track patterns and improve service quality across affected areas.
BESCOM complaint process and BESCOM electricity complaints registration optionsBESCOM accepts complaints through several channels. You can call the 24/7 helpline on 1912 and share your issue with a customer care representative.
Another option is the BESCOM website, using the "ONLINE GRIEVANCE REGISTRATION" section for electronic submissions.Using BESCOM complaint process through app and WhatsApp BESCOM electricity complaints registration.
Consumers may install the "BESCOM Mithra" app from the App Store or Google Play Store. After adding the BESCOM account by entering the consumer number or RR number, users can open the "Complaint Registration" menu. Complaints also reach BESCOM via WhatsApp on 9449844640. Always include your consumer number, describe the problem, and add any reference codes to help teams resolve cases faster.
Bengaluru Weather
Bengaluru is currently witnessing its peak temperature in a year. Minimum temperature in Bengaluru is likely to be close to 24 degree Celsius and the maximum temperature is likely to be close to 24 degree Celsius in Bengaluru on Tuesday. As per IMD report, Bengaluru is likely to witness "mainly clear sky with maximum and minimum temperature very likely to be around 36 ° C and 24 ° C respectively." The maximum temperature in the city is likely to remain above normal by 2-3 degree Celsius.

full_text:
Priya Pal checked into a hotel to escape the extreme heat and access air conditioning, as her family struggled to cope with rising temperatures at home.
Bengaluru Woman Books Hotel Room For AC Amid Heatwave, Sparks Debate
Bengaluru Heatwave AC Shortage: Bengaluru woman checks into hotel for AC as heat rises and coolers run out of stock; viral video highlights city’s struggle with soaring temperatures.
- Bengaluru resident books hotel room to escape city heat.
- AC and cooler stocks depleted, delivery times extended.
- Cooling shortage leads to creative, temporary solutions.
- Viral video highlights urban adaptation to extreme weather.
Bengaluru Heatwave AC Shortage: As temperatures soar in Bengaluru, one resident’s inventive workaround to escape the heat has struck a chord online, highlighting the growing strain on cooling resources in the city. A social media user, Priya Pal shared a now-viral video on Instagram showing how she checked into a nearby hotel—not for a vacation, but simply to access air conditioning for the day. Her post offers a glimpse into the practical challenges many households are facing amid rising temperatures and limited access to cooling appliances.
Bengaluru AC Shortage: A Hotel Stay To Escape Heat
In the video, Priya is seen relaxing indoors, away from the sweltering outdoor conditions. She explained that her family decided to book a hotel room next to their home after struggling to cope without adequate cooling.
Initially expecting rainfall to bring relief, the family instead encountered persistent heat. Attempts to purchase an air conditioner or cooler proved futile, as stocks had run out and delivery timelines stretched up to a week.
Cooling Shortage Forces Creative Solutions
Priya later shared in the comments that even rental options were unavailable due to high demand. With no immediate solution in sight, the family ordered a cooler online but opted to spend the weekend in a hotel room, enjoying the comfort of air conditioning.
“Looks like a good decision to me,” she wrote, describing their day spent indoors watching shows and avoiding the heat.
Her experience reflects a broader issue across Bengaluru, where demand for cooling appliances has surged sharply, leaving many residents scrambling for alternatives.
Social Media Reacts
The post quickly gained traction, with users relating to the situation and praising the workaround. Some called it a practical choice under the circumstances, while others responded with humour.
Comments ranged from agreement—“At this point, this actually makes sense”—to lighthearted takes like, “Netflix and chill with full AC sounds like a perfect Sunday.”
Several users also pointed out that air conditioners and coolers are currently out of stock in multiple parts of the city, reinforcing Priya’s experience.
A Glimpse Into A Larger Trend
While the video has a humorous undertone, it reflects a serious reality: residents are increasingly improvising to cope with rising heat levels. From short-term fixes like hotel stays to delayed appliance deliveries, the situation underscores how urban populations are adapting to extreme weather conditions.
